ip_outcome_note: Depicted long sinuous striped ferret-like creature leaping through autumn foliage matches Furret's canonical design exactly. National Pokedex no. 162. Normal-type Stage 1. Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-136).

trivia_bullets.text: Furret (National Pokedex no. 162) is a Normal-type Stage 1 introduced in Generation II (Gold & Silver). It evolves from Sentret at level 15 — Sentret was trivia'd in wave 12 for this archive. Furret completes that line. Both are Johto route-normal staples: Sentret as the lookout scout, Furret as the long-body climber. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-136]

Flavor text: "It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokemon to enter." — Furret's body-length is the defining survival adaptation: the nest is a personal-fit burrow no predator can follow into. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-136]

The Feelin' Fine attack (draw 3 cards for one Colorless energy) and Tail Smash (90 damage for one Colorless, coin-flip) give Furret an unusually efficient draw engine for a Colorless uncommon. Feelin' Fine in particular is a strong consistency tool in budget or theme-deck builds. Tail Smash's coin-flip risk is the trade-off for the high ceiling damage. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-136]

related_cards.name: Sentret
related_cards.note: Pre-evolution; wave 12 trivia'd in the DAA set

related_cards.name: Furret
related_cards.note: Multiple prior prints across older sets; long-running TCG presence since Gen 2
